section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's echocardiogram shows low-ish pumping function of the heart at about 45% and mitral regurgitation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The exact same echocardiogram finding is repeated in the Assessment section as part of the diagnostic impression. Having it also in O is redundant and blurs the distinction between raw data and synthesized assessment, though it is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient will continue on lisinopril.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a statement of the current medication regimen rather than a new change or explicit order; per instructions, ongoing medications belong in S unless framed as a treatment change or order.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor wants the patient to take lisinopril as directed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an explicit management instruction and belongs in P, but it is redundant with the prior statement about continuing lisinopril and could be misread as a new change rather than clarification of current therapy.
